channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Syrians begin fasting during first Ramadan without Assad family rule in decades
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Some restaurants and coffee shops in Syria were closed during the day Saturday while others opened as usual as observant Muslims began fasting during the holy month of Ramadan, the first since the fall of Assad family rule in the war-torn country.
item.dc:creator: ABDULRAHMAN SHAHEEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A topsy-turvy Oscar season concludes Sunday. Here's what to look for at Sunday's show
item.description: After a topsy-turvy Oscar season in which frontrunners were constantly shuffled, old tweets hobbled a top contender and space was held for “Wicked,” the 97th Academy Awards get underway Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Jake Coyle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Anti-DOGE protests at Tesla stores target Elon Musk's bottom line
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Demonstrators gathered outside Tesla stores across the U.S. Saturday to protest the automaker's billionaire CEO, Elon Musk, and his push to slash government spending on behalf of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: RODRIQUE NGOWI and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judge rules head of watchdog agency must keep his job, says Trump's bid to oust him was unlawful
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The head of a federal watchdog agency must remain in his job, a judge in Washington ruled on Saturday, saying President Donald Trump's bid to remove the special counsel was unlawful. U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: About 8 in 10 say they've recently experienced extreme weather: Survey
item.description: Overall, nearly 70 percent of adults believe in climate change, with 93 percent of Democrats and 51 percent of Republicans saying so, poll results show.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, KREX 5

item.title: Lifelong New York civil rights advocate and NAACP leader Hazel Dukes dies at 92
item.description: Hazel Dukes, the president of the New York State chapter of the NAACP and lifelong civil rights advocate, died Saturday at the age of 92. Dukes peacefully passed away in her New York City home surrounded by family, her son, Ronald Dukes, said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Charli xcx’s' Brat reign continues, as she clears the decks at BRIT Awards
item.description: Charli xcx’s “Brat” reign continues, as she cleared the decks at this year’s BRIT Awards – the U.K.’s leading music awards show.
item.dc:creator: SIAN WATSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge extends block on Trump gender-affirming care federal funding ban
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge Lauren King ruled that President Trump’s executive orders violate the Constitution’s Fifth Amendment, which grants equal protection under the law.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KREX 5

item.title: Cuomo jumps into New York mayor's race, attempting political comeback
item.description: Former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo (D) announced Saturday afternoon that he's entering New York City’s mayoral race, adding a big name to the already busy Democratic primary challenging Mayor Eric Adams (D).
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, Fox 5

item.title: Lee Kunzman, 'persistent' Indy 500 veteran, dies at 80
item.description: Kunzman's best finish in his INDYCAR SERIES career was in 1979, when he placed second at Atlanta Motor Speedway. It was just a narrow loss to winner Johnny Rutherford, IMS officials said.
item.dc:creator: Cooper Hudson, KREX 5

item.title: Singer Angie Stone killed in car crash after leaving Alabama performance
item.description: Stone was riding in the back of a vehicle that "flipped over and subsequently hit by a big rig," according to family and friends.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, KRON4

item.title: Threatening letter sent to northern Colorado family over MAGA banner
item.description: A Severance family says they recently received a threatening letter in the mail, in response to a "Make America Great Again" banner posted in their front yard.
item.dc:creator: Greg Nieto, KRON4

item.title: FedEx cargo plane makes emergency landing following bird strike and engine fire
item.description: A FedEx cargo plane made an emergency landing at a busy New Jersey airport on Saturday after a bird strike caused an engine fire that could be seen in the morning sky.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oscar presenters Emma Stone, Andrew Garfield, Whoopi Goldberg rehearse before the show
item.description: Emma Stone knows her way around the Dolby Theatre, but the even the two-time Oscar winner needs a little rehearsing before the big show. This year she’s not a nominee but a presenter, though which category she’ll be announcing is under wraps until it’s broadcast live around the world on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: For Gene Hackman, Santa Fe was a refuge from the limelight
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — The piñon- and juniper-scented Rocky Mountain foothills overlooking Santa Fe have helped the city earn a reputation as a refuge for famous actors and authors seeking to escape the spotlight.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Renowned geneticist Francis Collins retires from NIH, urging 'respect' for embattled workers
item.description: Dr. Francis Collins, a renowned geneticist, and former longtime director of the National Institutes of Health, is retiring from the government agency and urging “respect” for its embattled workers.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Singer Angie Stone killed in car crash after performance: Report
item.description: TMZ says per Stone's representative, Stone left a performance in Alabama early Saturday when she was killed in a car crash.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, NewsNation

item.title: David Johansen, singer from the seminal punk band the New York Dolls, dies at 75
item.description: David Johansen, the wiry, gravelly-voiced singer and last surviving member of the glam and protopunk band the New York Dolls who later performed as his campy, pompadoured alter ego, Buster Poindexter, has died. He was 75.
item.dc:creator: Mark Kennedy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Border czar makes the city of Boston his latest immigration target
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — As Mayor Michelle Wu prepares to go to Congress to defend Boston's protections of immigrant communities, the city is in a war of words with the Trump administration and Republican representatives over how much police should support deportations.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Is there a secret behind the Netflix intro sound? Some viewers seem to think so
item.description: The origin of the familiar sound has become a topic of conversation among online conspiracy theorists in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Princeton preppy murder suspect attempts suicide as slain brother laid to rest: report
item.description: A New Jersey man who is accused of sadistically killing his brother and trying to eat his eyeball attempted suicide inside Thursday, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: 2 window washers rescued from 'spinning' scaffolding in New York City
item.description: The wind took over a scaffolding while two workers were washing the windows of a high-rise building in Midtown.
item.dc:creator: Eliecer Marte, Fox 5

item.title: Zelensky won’t apologize for Oval Office spat, says it was bad for both sides
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky wouldn’t apologize for the meeting with President Trump, which erupted into a remarkable and tense spat, arguing that the situation was bad for both countries.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, ABC 4

item.title: 2 window washers rescued from 'spinning' scaffolding in Manhattan
item.description: The wind took over a scaffolding while two workers were washing the windows of a high-rise building in Midtown.
item.dc:creator: Eliecer Marte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge blocks Trump order threatening funding for institutions that offer care for transgender youth
item.description: A judge has continued to block President Donald Trump’s plan to pull federal funding from institutions that provide gender-affirming care for transgender youth.
item.dc:creator: Gene Johnson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jury finds Illinois landlord guilty of murder, hate crime in 2023 attack on Palestinian American boy
item.description: JOLIET, Ill. (AP) — A jury found an Illinois landlord guilty of murder and hate crime charges Friday for the brutal killing of a 6-year-old whose mother rented rooms in the man’s home, an attack that spiked fears over anti-Muslim discrimination in the earliest days of the war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and MELISSA PEREZ WINDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Menendez family criticizes LA district attorney for opposing brothers' new trial
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Cousins of Erik and Lyle Menendez spoke out Thursday criticizing the Los Angeles district attorney's recent decision to oppose a new trial for the brothers, who have spent nearly 30 years in prison for the 1989 killing of their parents.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, WGN 9
